---
title: "V. Jesus in Salvation — New Birth"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: false
    number-sections: false
    geometry: margin=1in
    fontsize: 11pt
    pdf-engine: xelatex
mainfont: "Arial"
---

# V. Jesus in Salvation — New Birth

[Opening Song](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3MZgXXUW08Q)

[Overview Video](https://bibleproject.com/videos/redemption/)


## Opening Passages
 [John 1:12–13](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A12-13&version=WEB) — "But as many as received him, to them he gave the right to become God's children, to those who believe in his name: who were born, not of blood, nor of the will of the flesh, nor of the will of man, but of God."

 [John 3:3–8](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+3%3A3-8&version=WEB) — "Unless one is born again he cannot see the kingdom of God… That which is born of the flesh is flesh, and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit."

 [1 Peter 1:12](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Peter+1%3A12&version=WEB) — "… spoke of the things that have now been told you by those who have preached the gospel to you by the Holy Spirit sent from heaven. Even angels long to look into these things."


## A. Key Greek Terms

### New Testament (Greek)

#### 1. Born again / from above — γεννηθῇ ἄνωθεν (gennēthē anōthen)
  • Born again or born from above

  • [John 3:3](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+3%3A3&version=WEB) — "Unless one is born again he cannot see the kingdom of God"


#### 2. Born of God — γεγέννηται ἐκ τοῦ θεοῦ (gegennētai ek tou theou)
  • Begotten of God

  • [1 John 5:1](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+John+5%3A1&version=WEB) — "Everyone who believes… has been born of God"


#### 3. Born again (verb) — ἀναγεννάω (anagennaō)
  • To beget again, cause to be born again

  • [1 Peter 1:3](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Peter+1%3A3&version=WEB) — "He has caused us to be born again to a living hope"


#### 4. Regeneration — παλιγγενεσία (palingenesia)
  • Rebirth, regeneration, new genesis

  • [Titus 3:5](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Titus+3%3A5&version=WEB) — "the washing of regeneration and renewal of the Holy Spirit"


#### 5. New creation — καινὴ κτίσις (kainē ktisis)
  • New creation

  • [2 Corinthians 5:17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2+Corinthians+5%3A17&version=WEB) — "If anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation"


#### 6. Made alive together — συζωοποιέω (syzōopoieō)
  • Made alive together with Christ

  • [Ephesians 2:5](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Ephesians+2%3A5&version=WEB) — "made us alive together with Christ"


## B. Old Testament Foundations

### Heart of Stone → Heart of Flesh

#### Ezekiel 36:25–27
  • [Ezekiel 36:25–27](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Ezekiel+36%3A25-27&version=WEB) — "I will give you a new heart, and a new spirit I will put within you. And I will remove the heart of stone from your flesh and give you a heart of flesh. And I will put my Spirit within you, and cause you to walk in my statutes…"


#### Ezekiel 11:19–20
  • [Ezekiel 11:19–20](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Ezekiel+11%3A19-20&version=WEB) — "I will give them one heart and a new spirit I will put within them. I will remove the heart of stone from their flesh and give them a heart of flesh…"


### New Covenant: Law Written on the Heart

#### Jeremiah 31:31–34
  • [Jeremiah 31:31–34](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Jeremiah+31%3A31-34&version=WEB) — "I will make a new covenant… I will put my law within them, and I will write it on their hearts… I will forgive their iniquity, and I will remember their sin no more."


#### Jeremiah 32:39–40
  • [Jeremiah 32:39–40](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Jeremiah+32%3A39-40&version=WEB) — "I will give them one heart and one way… I will put the fear of me in their hearts…"


### Circumcision of the Heart

#### Deuteronomy 30:6
  • [Deuteronomy 30:6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+30%3A6&version=WEB) — "The Lord your God will circumcise your heart… so that you will love the Lord your God with all your heart…"


#### Deuteronomy 10:16
  • [Deuteronomy 10:16](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+10%3A16&version=WEB) — "Circumcise therefore the foreskin of your heart…"


### New Life

#### Ezekiel 37 — Valley of Dry Bones
  • [Ezekiel 37](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Ezekiel+37&version=WEB) — God breathes life into dead Israel via His Spirit → they live.


#### Deuteronomy 30:6 — Moving from Spiritual Death to Life
  • [Deuteronomy 30:6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+30%3A6&version=WEB) and the "new heart" passages imply moving from spiritual death to life.


#### Psalm 80:18 — God Revives His People
  • [Psalm 80:18](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+80%3A18&version=WEB) — God revives His people.


### Other Key Passages
  • [Psalm 51:10](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Psalm+51%3A10&version=WEB) — "Create in me a clean heart, O God, and renew a right spirit within me."

  • [Jeremiah 24:7](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Jeremiah+24%3A7&version=WEB) — "I will give them a heart to know that I am the Lord…"


## C. New Birth / Born Again

### John 1:12–13
  • [John 1:12–13](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A12-13&version=WEB) — "…to all who did receive him… he gave the right to become children of God, who were born, not of blood nor of the will of the flesh nor of the will of man, but of God."


### John 3:3–8 — Jesus to Nicodemus
  • [John 3:3–8](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+3%3A3-8&version=WEB) — "Unless one is born again [γεννηθῇ ἄνωθεν] he cannot see the kingdom of God… That which is born of the Spirit is spirit."


### 1 Peter 1:3
  • [1 Peter 1:3](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Peter+1%3A3&version=WEB) — "He has caused us to be born again [ἀναγεννάω] to a living hope through the resurrection of Jesus Christ…"


### 1 Peter 1:23
  • [1 Peter 1:23](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Peter+1%3A23&version=WEB) — "Having been born again, not of perishable seed but of imperishable, through the living and abiding word of God."


### 1 John — Born of God
  • [1 John 5:1](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+John+5%3A1&version=WEB) — "Everyone who believes… has been born of God"

  • [1 John 3:9](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+John+3%3A9&version=WEB) — "No one born of God makes a practice of sinning"

  • [1 John 4:7](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+John+4%3A7&version=WEB) — "Everyone who loves has been born of God"


## D. New Creation

### 2 Corinthians 5:17
  • [2 Corinthians 5:17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=2+Corinthians+5%3A17&version=WEB) — "If anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation [καινὴ κτίσις]. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come."


### Galatians 6:15
  • [Galatians 6:15](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+6%3A15&version=WEB) — "Neither circumcision nor uncircumcision means anything; what counts is a new creation."


## E. Made Alive with Christ

### Ephesians 2:1–10
  • [Ephesians 2:1–10](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Ephesians+2%3A1-10&version=WEB) — "You were dead in the trespasses and sins… But God… made us alive together with Christ… For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works."


### Colossians 2:13
  • [Colossians 2:13](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Colossians+2%3A13&version=WEB) — "You, who were dead in your sins… God made you alive together with him."


### Romans 6:4
  • [Romans 6:4](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Romans+6%3A4&version=WEB) — "Just as Christ was raised from the dead… we too might walk in newness of life."


### Titus 3:5
  • [Titus 3:5](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Titus+3%3A5&version=WEB) — "He saved us… by the washing of regeneration [παλιγγενεσία] and renewal of the Holy Spirit."


## F. Jesus a Kinsman-Redeemer

### What Is a Kinsman-Redeemer?
  In ancient Israel, a kinsman-redeemer was a close male relative who had the responsibility (and privilege) to step in and restore what was lost for a family member in need.

 [Leviticus 25:25–28](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Leviticus+25%3A25-28&version=WEB) — Redeeming property sold due to poverty

 [Leviticus 25:47–49](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Leviticus+25%3A47-49&version=WEB) — Redeeming a relative sold into slavery

 [Deuteronomy 25:5–10](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Deuteronomy+25%3A5-10&version=WEB) — Marrying a childless widow to preserve the family name (levirate marriage; see also the Book of Ruth)


### Jesus Meets Every Requirement

#### 1. He became our Kinsman — by taking on human flesh, Jesus is truly one of us
  [John 1:14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+1%3A14&version=WEB) — "The Word became flesh and dwelt among us."

  [Hebrews 2:14–17](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Hebrews+2%3A14-17&version=WEB) — "Since the children have flesh and blood, he too shared in their humanity… He had to be made like his brothers in every way, in order that he might become a merciful and faithful high priest…"


#### 2. He was able to pay the price — only a sinless God-man could redeem us from sin's debt
  [1 Peter 1:18–19](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=1+Peter+1%3A18-19&version=WEB) — "You were redeemed… not with perishable things such as silver or gold, but with the precious blood of Christ, a lamb without blemish or defect."


#### 3. He was willing — Jesus laid down His life voluntarily
  [John 10:17–18](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=John+10%3A17-18&version=WEB) — "I lay down my life… No one takes it from me, but I lay it down of my own accord."

  [Galatians 3:13](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+3%3A13&version=WEB) — "Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law by becoming a curse for us."


### Key Verses on Jesus and Redemption
 [Ephesians 1:7](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Ephesians+1%3A7&version=WEB) — "In him we have redemption through his blood, the forgiveness of sins, in accordance with the riches of God's grace."

 [Colossians 1:13–14](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Colossians+1%3A13-14&version=WEB) — "He has rescued us from the dominion of darkness and brought us into the kingdom of the Son he loves, in whom we have redemption, the forgiveness of sins."

 [Revelation 5:9](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+5%3A9&version=WEB) — "You are worthy… because you were slain, and with your blood you purchased for God persons from every tribe and language and people and nation."

 [Isaiah 53:5–6](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Isaiah+53%3A5-6&version=WEB) — "He was pierced for our transgressions… the punishment that brought us peace was on him." (prophetic of the Messiah)

 [Galatians 4:4–5](https://www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Galatians+4%3A4-5&version=WEB) — "God sent his Son, born of a woman… to redeem those under the law, that we might receive adoption to sonship."

Jesus doesn't just rescue us from sin's penalty — He restores our inheritance, adopts us into God's family, and gives us a new name and future. Like Boaz with Ruth, He brings outsiders (Gentiles included) into the covenant family.
